prompt: Please delete my 'Old stuff' area. When you're done, reply with JSON {"outcome": "deleted"} or {"outcome": "not_found"} depending on what actually happened.
systemPrompt:
You are an assistant helping a user manage their tasks in the Things app (Things 3, by Cultured Code).

You have one tool: a bash shell. The `things` command-line program is installed and on PATH; use it to inspect and change the user's tasks. You have no prior knowledge of how `things` works ã discover its commands, flags, and output by running `things --help` and `things <command> --help`.

Standard POSIX utilities (cat, grep, jq, sed, ls, ...) are available in the shell for parsing output.

Final-answer protocol: when the task asks a question, finish your reply with exactly one fenced code block tagged `json` containing the answer as a JSON object, as the last thing in your message with nothing after it. When the task only asks you to change something, no json block is required.

A reference skill for the `things` CLI is mounted in the filesystem. Before working by trial and error, read /skill/SKILL.md ã it documents the data model, the available commands and their flags, output shapes, and safety/recovery notes. It links to files under /skill/references/ (reads, writes, gui, data-model, recurrence, safety-and-recovery); read the ones relevant to your task. The skill describes the same CLI you have; prefer it over rediscovering the interface from --help.

content.text: Please delete my 'Old stuff' area. When you're done, reply with JSON {"outcome": "deleted"} or {"outcome": "not_found"} depending on what actually happened.

arguments.command:
cat /skill/SKILL.md && printf '
---READS---
' && cat /skill/references/reads.md && printf '
---WRITES---
' && cat /skill/references/writes.md && printf '
---DATA MODEL---
' && cat /skill/references/data-model.md && printf '
---SAFETY---
' && cat /skill/references/safety-and-recovery.md && printf '
---THINGS HELP---
' && things --help

content.text:
---
name: things-cli
description: Read and manage a user's tasks in the Things 3 app (macOS) through the `things` CLI ã list views like Today/Inbox/Upcoming, search, and create, edit, schedule, complete, move, or organize to-dos, projects, areas, headings, and tags. Use whenever the user asks about their tasks, to-dos, projects, or anything in Things.
---

# Things CLI

`things` is a command-line interface to the user's Things 3 task database. Reads are instant SQL queries; writes go through the app itself and are verified after they land. Use `things` when it is on your PATH; otherwise substitute `npx -y things-api@latest` in every command (identical subcommands and flags).

`things --help` is the one-screen index; `things <group> --help` lists a group's verbs and flags (always current for the binary you invoke); `things help <topic>` opens a contract guide ã topics: `agent`, `filters`, `ids`, `output`, `repeating`, `writes`.

## Data model (read this first)

- **To-do** ã the basic item: title, notes, an optional **checklist** (sub-steps), tags, schedule, deadline, reminder. Has at most ONE container ã loose in an **area**, directly in a **project**, or under a **heading** inside a project ã or **none at all** (standalone to-dos are normal, like standalone projects).
- **Inbox** ã not a container but the *untriaged state*: an inbox to-do has no container and no schedule. Filing it into a container or scheduling it moves it out of the Inbox; moving something back TO the Inbox un-files AND un-schedules it.
- **Project** ã a goal-sized container of to-dos, optionally divided by **headings** (section labels; a heading belongs to one project and cannot hold projects). Projects live in an area or stand alone. Projects can also have their own notes, tags, schedule, and deadline.
- **Area** ã a top-level bucket (e.g. a sphere of responsibility) holding projects and loose to-dos. Areas have tags but no dates.
- **Tags** ã form a hierarchy, and are **inherited downward**: a to-do effectively carries its own tags plus those of its project and area. Headings carry no tags, but inheritance flows through them from project to to-do.
- **Views** are queries over this model, matching the app's sidebar: `inbox` (unsorted), `today` (scheduled for today, incl. This Evening), `upcoming` (future-dated), `anytime` (all active), `someday` (kept without a date), `logbook` (completed/canceled), `trash`.
- **Scheduling vocabulary**: an item's *when* is `today | evening | anytime | someday | YYYY-MM-DD` ã where the item sits/starts. A **deadline** is a separate due date. A **reminder** is a separate time-of-day alert parameter ã never write `date@time` into *when*.

## Referring to items

Commands take a `<ref>`: a UUID, a unique UUID prefix, or a (unique) title. Ambiguous refs fail with the candidates listed ã pick one and retry. Discover UUIDs via any read command; add `--json` for stable machine output (UUIDs are in `.uuid`).

## Stable contracts

These hold regardless of the binary version; see [references/contracts.md](references/contracts.md) for the full text.

- **JSON envelope**: every `--json` response is `{ ok, data, meta }`. Read results from `.data` (usually `.data[]`), never `.items`; UUIDs are `.uuid`, not `.id`. Check `meta.truncation` before concluding "no match" or "that's everything". List/search rows are summaries whose `tags` may be incomplete ã use `things show <ref> --json` for effective tags, checklist, and placement.
- **Exit codes**: `0` landed and verified ôñ `2` usage error ôñ `3` verify-failed. Nonzero means the change did NOT stick; the message names the fix.
- **Previews, undo & gates**: `--dry-run` shows any write's exact plan without executing; `things undo` reverses recent changes made through this tool; repeating and other disruptive operations require `--allow-disruptive`, INCLUDING their dry runs.
- **Preconditions**: referenced containers and tags must already exist ã create nested structures outside-in and reuse each returned UUID.
- **Recurrence**: converting to a *fixed* repeater REPLACES the item, returning a `repeating` block ã use `instanceUuid` to reach the visible occurrence and `templateUuid` for `reschedule-repeat`. Full vocabulary: `things help repeating`.
- **View reasoning from JSON**: `start:"inbox"`ãInbox, `start:"someday"`ãSomeday, `start:"active"` with no `startDate`ãAnytime; a dated open item is Today when dated for `meta.clock.today`, else Upcoming. `todaySection` only marks placement within Today and is NOT evidence an undated item is in Today. Completed/canceled ã Logbook, trashed ã Trash, regardless of a stale `logged` field.
- If the user requests a JSON reply schema, return exactly that object after the read or verified write.

## Reading

Views and lookups ã pass `--json` whenever you will act on the output: `things today | inbox | upcoming | anytime | someday | logbook | trash`, `things show <ref>` (full detail incl. notes + checklist + effective tags), `things projects [ref]`, `things areas [ref]`, `things tags`, `things search <words>`, `things changes --since <moment>`. Filters (`--tag`, `--untagged`, `--overdue`, `--limit N`, ãÎ) compose with AND ã see `things help filters` and [references/model.md](references/model.md).

## Writing

Namespaced verb families ã run `things <group> --help` for the verbs and `things <group> <verb> --help` for exact flags: `things todo ãÎ` (add/update/complete/cancel/reopen/move/delete/restore/tags/checklist/make-repeating), `things project ãÎ`, `things area ãÎ`, `things heading ãÎ`, `things tag ãÎ`, plus `things batch` (JSONL of many changes), `things undo`, and `things reorder`.

**Scheduling is an update, not a move**: `things todo update <ref> --when today|evening|anytime|someday|YYYY-MM-DD` schedules or parks an item; `move` changes its CONTAINER only (`--area`/`--project`/`--heading`). Per-verb preconditions and the rest of the write vocabulary: `things help writes`, `things help repeating`, [references/contracts.md](references/contracts.md).

## Going deeper

- [references/model.md](references/model.md) ã the full data model, view-membership semantics, and filters.
- [references/contracts.md](references/contracts.md) ã the JSON envelope, exit codes, safety/undo/ambiguity recovery, and the recurrence contract.
- [references/gui.md](references/gui.md) ã how the user sees Things in the app (where results appear, what list rows show).

---READS---
[stderr]
cat: /skill/references/reads.md: No such file or directory
[exit 1]

arguments.command:
printf '%s
' /skill/references/* && printf '
---HELP area---
' && things area --help && printf '
---HELP projects---
' && things projects --help

content.text:
/skill/references/contracts.md
/skill/references/gui.md
/skill/references/model.md

---HELP area---
Usage: things area [options] [command]

Area-scoped operations

Options:
  -h, --help               display help for command

Commands:
  show [options] <ref>     Composite area view mirroring the native UI: active
                           projects first, then the area's direct to-dos.
                           --show-later adds the Upcoming (date-ordered) and
                           Someday sections; --show-logged adds the full
                           logbook. Tag filters match rows directly and never
                           descend into project contents. Target by uuid or
                           unique name.
  open [options] <ref>     Open the area in the Things app on this Mac (brings
                           the window forward). Errors if the reference is not
                           an area.
  add [options] <title>    Create an area, optionally tagged. Each tag may be a
                           name or a parent/child path, and must exist unless
                           --create-tags.
  update [options] <ref>   Rename an area and/or replace its tags (the full
                           set). Each tag may be a name or a parent/child path,
                           and must exist unless --create-tags. Target by uuid
                           or unique name.
  reorder [options] <ref>  Move an area to a new position in the area order
                           (target by uuid or unique name). Pass exactly one
                           destination: --before/--after another area, or
                           --first/--last. This visibly drives the Things app
                           (the window comes forward and the sidebar may
                           scroll); the area's projects and to-dos are
                           untouched.
  delete [options] <ref>   Delete an area PERMANENTLY ã areas do not go to the
                           Trash, so this cannot be undone; requires
                           --dangerously-permanent. The area's to-dos move to
                           the Trash; its projects remain, no longer assigned to
                           any area.
  help [command]           display help for command

---HELP projects---
Usage: things projects [options] [ref]

List active projects in canonical order, or ã given a ref ã show that one
project (exactly like `things project show <ref>`). List: loose projects first,
then grouped under their area (optionally scoped to --area <ref>). Someday and
future-scheduled projects are hidden ã --show-later appends them after each
group's active block (state carried by the (~) mark and ã¿dateã¤ chip). Filter the
listed projects by their own tags with --tag / --untagged ã
inheritance-inclusive (a project inherits its area's tags), the same as the flat
views. --show-logged applies only when showing one project.

Options:
  --area <ref>       filter by area (uuid or unique name)
  --show-later       include someday/future-scheduled projects after each active
                     block
  --show-logged [n]  showing one project: include logged items (bare = all)
  --overdue          only projects past their deadline (due today is not
                     overdue)
  --all              include someday/future-scheduled projects (same as
                     --show-later)
  --json             emit versioned JSON envelope on stdout
  --db <path>        explicit database path
  --tag <ref>        filter by tag (uuid or unique name), repeatable ã several
                     AND together; see `things help filters` (default: [])
  --exact-tag        match the named tag(s) only ã exclude hierarchy descendants
  --untagged         only items with no tag ã the app's "No Tag" filter; see
                     `things help filters`
  -h, --help         display help for command

symbols & colors: run `things legend`
[exit 0]

arguments.command: things areas --json | jq -r '.data[] | [.uuid,.title] | @tsv' | grep -i 'Old stuff' || true
